Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=3-(aminomethyl)indole + 2 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = gramine + 2 S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 2 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:52264, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:136514, ChEBI:CHEBI:136516; EC=2.1.1.340; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:16664431, ECO:0000269|PubMed:16930646};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Methylates 3-aminomethylindole (AMI) and N-methyl-3-aminomethylindole (MAMI), two substrates involved in gramine biosynthesis, a toxic indole alkaloid. Can use S-adenosyl-L-methionine (AdoMet) as a methyl donor. Unable to mediate caffeic acid O-methylation. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:16930646}.
